status: In progress — Stage 1 validated through epoch 7. V6-Extended curriculum finalised and ready for resume from best.pt (epoch 7).
motivation: V5 proved the geometric architecture (bottleneck projector + block-diagonal mask + SAM Orth LoRA) achieves 0.81 Oracle mIoU on RWTD, but suffered catastrophic language collapse (0.14 live inference) because loss masking zeroed the LM signal on all text tokens. V6 introduces a Proximity-Decayed LM Loss to provide per-token linguistic regularisation without reverting to V3's Count-Collapse regime, plus a staged Double-Decay curriculum to prevent Latent Co-Adaptation Churn between a fast-moving projector and slow-moving LoRAs.

architecture.qwen: Qwen3-VL-8B + LoRA (r=8, q_proj + v_proj). Ultra-conservative qwen_lr_scale=0.01 (1e-6 effective).
architecture.seg_token: <|seg|> appended after each TEXTURE description. Dedicated grounding anchor (inherited from V5).
architecture.block_diagonal_mask: 4D attention mask decouples textures (inherited from V5).
architecture.projector: Bottleneck 4096 -> 512 -> 256 with LayerNorm + GELU + Dropout(0.15). ~2.1M params (inherited from V4-Slim/V5).
architecture.sam3: Batch Multiplexed + Orthogonal LoRA r=32 on q,k,v,out_proj (inherited from V5).
architecture.v6_new_1_proximity_decayed_lm_loss: Instead of masking all text tokens to -100 (V5 regime, caused LoRA catastrophic forgetting), V6 weights every token's CE contribution by its cosine proximity to the nearest <|seg|> position. Tokens immediately around [SEG] get near-full weight (linguistic anchoring); far-away filler tokens get near-zero weight (no pressure to collapse). Implemented via weighted_lm_loss(qwen_logits, labels, lm_weights).
architecture.v6_new_2_double_decay_curriculum: Three-stage curriculum with TWO projector LR decays: at Stage 2 entry the projector base LR drops 10x (1e-4 -> 1e-5) so Qwen LoRA can fine-tune without chasing a moving target; at Stage 3 entry another 10x drop (1e-5 -> 1e-6) so SAM LoRA can co-adapt under a near-frozen projector.
architecture.v6_new_3_orthogonal_clamp_stage3: orthogonal_weight_override=0.1 (10x the baseline 0.01) activates when SAM LoRA unfreezes, forcing SAM weights to stay close to pretrained.
architecture.v6_new_4_live_inference_test_eval: Periodic RWTD evaluation during training uses the two-pass inference_forward() path (Qwen generates, block-diagonal mask reconstructed on generated tokens) instead of V5's Oracle teacher-forced path. Tracks true end-to-end performance on OOD data.

stage_1_projector_warmup.description: Projector trains alone at base LR 1e-4. Qwen LoRA + SAM LoRA frozen. Goal: reach geometric potential before any LoRA gradient enters the system.
stage_1_projector_warmup.rationale: V5 and v6_completed showed the projector needs ~7-10 epochs to saturate. Introducing Qwen earlier (as V5's Stage 2@ep3 did) causes the projector to compete with an incompletely-warmed mapping.

stage_2_qwen_sync_first_decay.description: Qwen LoRA unfrozen at base * 0.01 = 1e-6. Projector base LR decayed 10x (1e-4 -> 1e-5). SAM LoRA still frozen.
stage_2_qwen_sync_first_decay.rationale: With the projector slowed down, Qwen can adjust [SEG] output distribution without a moving downstream target (addresses v6_completed's Latent Co-Adaptation Churn).

stage_3_full_e2e_second_decay.description: SAM LoRA unfrozen at base * 0.1 = 1e-5 with orthogonal_weight_override=0.1. Projector base LR decayed ANOTHER 10x (1e-5 -> 1e-6). Qwen LoRA continues at 1e-6.
stage_3_full_e2e_second_decay.rationale: Full three-component co-training with all components at extreme micro-rates. The cumulative 100x projector decay neutralises its tendency to drift, letting SAM and Qwen make the final geometric adjustments.

training_history_current_run.phase_across_ep1_7: All Stage 1 (Qwen LoRA frozen, SAM LoRA frozen)

rwtd_live_inference_ablation.description: Four-way evaluation performed 2026-04-16 to compare epoch 5 vs epoch 7 under two prompt conditions. Pulled from live inference_forward() two-pass pipeline on 253 RWTD samples.

A_standard_1_to_6.prompt: This image contains exactly 1 to 6 main visually distinct regions...
A_standard_1_to_6.note: Model spontaneously over-segments (195/253 predict k=6). Hungarian matching allows unused predicted channels, inflating aggregate mIoU.

B_exactly_2.prompt: This image contains exactly 2 main visually distinct regions...
B_exactly_2.note: Fixed k=2. No Hungarian slack. True baseline for semantic quality.

ep7_vs_ep5_standard.interpretation: Marginal gain — training still productive, no overfitting

ep7_vs_ep5_exactly_2.interpretation: Meaningful gain under constrained prompt — ep7 more prompt-robust

exactly2_vs_std_ep5.interpretation: Forcing k=2 hurts by 7.4 mIoU at ep5

exactly2_vs_std_ep7.interpretation: Forcing k=2 hurts by only 4.5 mIoU at ep7 — robustness gap

rwtd_live_inference_ablation.identical_k_distribution_finding: ep5 and ep7 produce the exact same k_pred distribution under the standard prompt {6:195, 2:46, 3:10, 4:1, 1:1}. This proves Qwen's generation behaviour is unchanged across epochs — all the +0.004 mIoU gain at ep7 comes from projector / mask-head refinement downstream. The V6 Proximity-Decayed LM Loss is working as designed: language output is stable.

oracle_vs_live_inversion.phenomenon: In V5, Oracle (0.81) massively exceeded Live (0.14) — indicating language collapse. In V6, the relationship INVERTS: Live (0.7356) exceeds Oracle (0.6022) at epoch 5.
oracle_vs_live_inversion.mechanism_1_hungarian_inflation: Live inference predicts k=6 for 195/253 samples. Hungarian matching picks the best 2 of 6 predicted channels against 2 GT classes. Extra channels are free mistakes that don't enter the matched mean. Oracle uses k_pred=k_gt=2, so every predicted segment is scored.
oracle_vs_live_inversion.mechanism_2_mask_reconstruction: The two-pass inference path rebuilds the block-diagonal attention mask from generated tokens, which can produce slightly cleaner [SEG] hidden states than the teacher-forced training path on OOD RWTD samples.
oracle_vs_live_inversion.empirical_proof: Forcing k=2 via the 'exactly 2' prompt drops live inference from 0.7356 to 0.6617 (-0.074) at ep5 — within 0.06 mIoU of the Oracle number (0.6022). The residual gap is attributable to mask reconstruction (mechanism 2). Hungarian inflation (mechanism 1) accounts for the majority (~80%) of the Oracle<->Live delta.

language_health.verdict: Language collapse FIXED by Proximity-Decayed LM Loss.

lm_loss_trajectory.v5: 21.77 -> 10.1 -> 7.66 (LoRA drift signature — large drops indicate aggressive LoRA adaptation away from pretrained distribution)
lm_loss_trajectory.v6_stage1: 3.67 flat across 7 epochs (Qwen LoRA frozen, projector-only training)
lm_loss_trajectory.v6_completed_full_run: 3.67 -> 1.32 over 16 epochs (healthy linguistic adaptation once Qwen unfroze, no collapse signature)
lm_loss_trajectory.verdict: V6 LM loss scale is smaller (weighted) and trajectory is gradual. No drift-into-garbage.

geometric_quality.verdict: V6's Oracle number is lower because Stage 1 training is slower-converging (projector-only, no Qwen gradient yet). V6 projects its upper bound at the peak of Stage 2-3 co-training, not Stage 1 Oracle.

what_we_learned.finding: Proximity-Decayed LM Loss completely prevents the V5 language collapse
what_we_learned.evidence: Live inference mIoU at ep5 is 0.7356 (V6) vs 0.1364 (V5). k_pred distribution is healthy (no collapse to k=0). Qwen generates coherent TEXTURE_N lines on every sample.
what_we_learned.impact: The V5 dilemma (100% masking -> LoRA forgets language; 0% masking -> count-collapse) is resolved. Partial weighted masking threads the needle.

what_we_learned.finding: Hungarian-matched mIoU with unconstrained k inflates scores by ~0.05-0.10
what_we_learned.evidence: ep7 standard prompt (k_pred up to 6) yields 0.7394. Same checkpoint with forced k=2 yields 0.6943. Delta = -0.045.
what_we_learned.impact: Public mIoU numbers must specify the prompt constraint. The 'exactly K' variant is the true semantic-quality metric; the 'up to N' variant measures the same model's scoring profile under over-segmentation.

what_we_learned.finding: Live inference > Oracle when language is healthy
what_we_learned.evidence: V6 ep5: Live 0.7356, Oracle 0.6022. Opposite sign from V5's 0.81/0.14 ratio.
what_we_learned.impact: The 'Oracle as upper bound' framing is V5-specific. For V6, Oracle is a conservative lower bound on live behaviour; live inference with Hungarian matching is optimistic. The 'exactly K' live result is the honest middle.

what_we_learned.finding: Qwen generation behaviour is stable across Stage 1 epochs
what_we_learned.evidence: Identical k_pred distribution at ep5 and ep7 under the standard prompt.
what_we_learned.impact: Improvements during Stage 1 come entirely from the projector and mask head, not from any change in Qwen output. This is the expected behaviour when Qwen LoRA is frozen and validates that upstream token representations are stable.

what_we_learned.finding: Longer Stage 1 appears to improve prompt robustness
what_we_learned.evidence: ep7 (later in Stage 1) degrades less under the 'exactly 2' constraint than ep5 (-4.5 vs -7.4 mIoU).
what_we_learned.impact: Motivated the V6-Extended curriculum change: projector_warmup_epochs raised from 7 to 10. More Stage 1 time = more robust representations before Qwen gradients arrive.

what_we_learned.finding: Latent Co-Adaptation Churn is a real failure mode
what_we_learned.evidence: v6_completed (16 epochs, single decay at Stage 2) showed RWTD Oracle regression ep10=0.713 -> ep15=0.678 during Stage 3. Projector at 1e-4 continued shifting the feature distribution while SAM LoRA at 1e-5 tried to track.
what_we_learned.impact: Motivated the V6-Extended second projector decay at Stage 3 (1e-5 -> 1e-6). Cumulative 100x reduction from base neutralises the hyperactive projector during SAM fine-tuning.

change_1_extend_stage_1.reason: ep5 vs ep7 ablation showed prompt robustness still improving at ep7. More projector-only time should close the gap to v5_oracle's ~0.81 Oracle ceiling before Qwen gradients enter.

change_2_second_projector_decay.from: Single 10x decay at Stage 2 (v6_completed / v6b design)
change_2_second_projector_decay.to: Double decay: 10x at Stage 2 AND 10x at Stage 3
change_2_second_projector_decay.reason: v6_completed showed RWTD regression during Stage 3 — classic Latent Co-Adaptation Churn. Dropping projector to 1e-6 during SAM unfreezing makes the projector effectively frozen so SAM can converge against a stable feature map.

change_3_stage_3_shifted_later.reason: Gives Stage 2 (Qwen sync) 4 epochs instead of 2. Qwen LoRA needs time to align its [SEG] output distribution with the projector's expectations before SAM joins the party.

change_4_live_test_eval.from: TestEvaluator uses model.forward() with teacher-forced GT text (Oracle)
change_4_live_test_eval.to: TestEvaluator uses model.inference_forward() with the standard '1 to 6' prompt (Live)
change_4_live_test_eval.reason: Oracle hides both the strengths (Hungarian-matched live score) and the weaknesses (language drift) of the end-to-end pipeline. Live inference is the honest monitoring signal. Over-segmentation is tracked via per-sample k_pred distribution and reported transparently.

v5_oracle_ep7.note: Teacher-forced; architecture ceiling but language broken

v6_live_ep7_standard.note: Current V6 checkpoint at end of Stage 1

v6_live_ep7_exactly_2.note: True semantic quality — no Hungarian slack

next_experiments.expected_trajectory: Epochs 8-10 complete Stage 1 (still projector-only). Epoch 11 fires Stage 2 transition (Qwen unfreeze + first decay). Epoch 15 fires Stage 3 transition (SAM unfreeze + second decay). Test eval at ep10 and ep15 gives uninflated RWTD checkpoints.

hypotheses_to_test.h1: Extended Stage 1 (10 epochs) will push RWTD live-standard mIoU above 0.75 (vs current 0.739 at ep7)
hypotheses_to_test.success_criterion: Epoch 10 live-standard mIoU > 0.75 AND exactly-2 mIoU > 0.70

hypotheses_to_test.h2: Double projector decay eliminates the v6_completed Stage 3 regression
hypotheses_to_test.success_criterion: Epoch 18 RWTD live-standard mIoU >= Epoch 15 (no regression); exactly-2 stable or improving

hypotheses_to_test.h3: Language coherence preserved end-to-end — no Chinese chars, no emojis, <|seg|> emitted on every sample
hypotheses_to_test.success_criterion: k_pred distribution at epoch 18 similar to epoch 7 under standard prompt; k_pred=0 samples < 1%
